us-politics.headline: Trump Warns Iran as US Jets Downed, Tensions Escalate
us-politics.body: Iran shot down US fighter jets, prompting Trump to warn that 'time is running out' before 'all hell' follows. World leaders are already moving to manage the Strait of Hormuz crisis without waiting for Washington, a sign of eroding US diplomatic leverage. Rep. Seth Moulton said bluntly that 'Iran is winning the war.' Former National Security Advisor John Bolton suggested Trump may be in 'panic mode.' The episode raises hard questions about US deterrence in the region and whether the administration has a coherent strategy beyond public threats.

sources.title: Trump warns Iran: ‘Time is running out’ before ‘all hell’ rains down
Moulton says ‘Iran is winning the war’ after US jets downed
Bolton: Trump likely in ‘panic mode’ after Iran attacks US fighter jets
World leaders bypass Trump to tackle Strait of Hormuz crisis

us-politics.headline: Trump Budget Boosts Defense, Slashes Domestic Spending
us-politics.body: The White House released a budget proposal seeking a $1.5 trillion increase in defense spending while cutting deeply across domestic programs. The plan includes $152 million to reopen Alcatraz as a high-security federal prison. Critics argue the cuts will hit services that middle- and working-class Americans rely on most, and at least one analysis warns the US economy is already sliding toward a serious recession driven by declining middle-class purchasing power. Supporters say the defense buildup is overdue and that domestic spending has grown unsustainable.

sources.title: Trump unveils $1.5T defense surge, deep domestic cuts — what’s on the budget chopping block
America is heading for a recession — and it may be the worst yet
Alcatraz could reopen as a 'state-of-the-art secure prison' under Trump's $152M budget request

us-politics.headline: Cabinet Shake-Ups and SCOTUS Birthright Case Rattle Washington
us-politics.body: The departures of Pam Bondi and Kristi Noem from the Cabinet have fueled speculation about further shake-ups, with questions mounting over internal White House stability. Meanwhile, the Supreme Court is weighing birthright citizenship, a case that could reshape immigration law. One opinion piece argues a constitutional amendment may ultimately be needed to settle the question. NPR's political roundup noted the week exposed tensions across the administration on multiple fronts simultaneously.

sources.title: Week in Politics: Trump's latest speech; SCOTUS on birthright citizenship; Pam Bondi
Bondi, Noem ousters raise questions about future Cabinet shake-ups
Reclaiming our own birthright: We might need to amend the Constitution

us-politics.headline: Virginia Redistricting Poll, Wyoming Race Signal 2026 Battle Lines
us-politics.body: A narrow majority of Virginia voters support redrawing congressional maps to favor Democrats, according to a new poll - a finding that reflects how redistricting has become a direct partisan tool on both sides. In Wyoming, the son of a prominent Republican megadonor has entered the race for an open at-large House seat, signaling that donor networks are moving early to shape the 2026 midterm landscape. Both stories point to a cycle where structural advantages, not just candidates, will determine control of Congress.

sources.title: Narrow majority in Virginia backs redistricting to favor Democrats: Poll
Son of Republican megadonor throws hat in the ring for open at-large House seat in Wyoming

au-politics.headline: Pezzullo found 'reckless' in dealings with Liberal powerbroker
au-politics.body:
A confidential report found former Home Affairs secretary Michael Pezzullo acted recklessly in his communications with Liberal powerbroker Scott Briggs, adding detail to the conduct that led to his sacking in 2023. The report's findings go beyond what was previously public, suggesting Pezzullo's engagement crossed lines expected of a senior public servant.

Pezzullo has contested aspects of the findings against him. The case raises ongoing questions about the boundaries between senior bureaucrats and political operatives, and whether existing accountability mechanisms are strong enough to catch such conduct early.

sources.title: Confidential report found former home affairs boss Michael Pezzullo was ‘reckless’ in engagement with Liberal powerbroker

au-politics.headline: Two arrested in Victoria over Dezi Freeman investigation
au-politics.body:
Victoria Police have arrested two people in connection with the Dezi Freeman investigation, with the arrests made in Porepunkah. Few details about the charges or the suspects have been released publicly at this stage.

The Freeman case has drawn sustained attention in Victoria. Police have not yet confirmed whether further arrests are expected.

sources.title: Victoria police arrest two people as part of Dezi Freeman investigation

world.headline: Iran-Israel War Escalates With Strikes on Nuclear Site, Universities
world.body:
Israel struck a university in Tehran and launched projectiles near Iran's Bushehr nuclear plant, killing one person, according to the IAEA. Iranian debris from intercepted projectiles landed inside Israel, setting vehicles ablaze. Tehran responded with sharp rhetoric, saying the US and Israel "belong in the Stone Age."

Israel also executed two members of a banned Iranian opposition group amid the fighting. A missing US airman adds a further flashpoint - rescue teams describe a "harrowing and dangerous" search inside Iranian territory. The conflict is widening fast, with no diplomatic off-ramp visible.

sources.title: Iran says US, Israel belong in Stone Age after Tehran university strike
Vehicles burn in Israel after being hit with Iranian projectile debris
Projectile hits near Iran’s Bushehr nuclear plant, killing one: IAEA
What we know so far about the search for missing US airman in Iran
Rescue team in Iran face 'harrowing and dangerous' search for US crew member
Iran executes two convicted members of banned opposition group

world.headline: Israel Advances Into Lebanon, Strikes Hospital in Tyre
world.body:
Israeli forces pushed deeper into southern Lebanon as strikes damaged a hospital in the coastal city of Tyre. Witnesses recorded buildings hit in residential areas. The ground invasion is advancing despite international pressure to halt.

The strikes mark a significant expansion of Israeli military operations beyond Gaza. Lebanon's health infrastructure is taking direct hits, raising urgent concerns from aid organizations.

sources.title: Witness records Israeli strike on building in Lebanon’s Tyre
Israeli strikes damage hospital in Lebanon’s Tyre; ground invasion advances
Israel’s unchecked war machine

world.headline: Russia Kills Five in Easter Market Strike, Zelensky Condemns Escalation
world.body:
A Russian attack on a Ukrainian market killed five people over the Easter weekend, with at least four more killed in separate strikes across the country. President Zelensky accused Moscow of choosing "Easter escalation" over a proposed ceasefire, pointing to the timing as deliberate.

The attacks undercut recent diplomatic signals and harden Kyiv's position that Russia is not negotiating in good faith.

sources.title: Russian attack on Ukraine market kills five
At least four people killed in Russian attacks on Ukraine
Russia chose 'Easter escalation' over ceasefire, says Zelensky

world.headline: Artemis II Crew Reaches Moon's Halfway Point, Sends Back Earth Images
world.body:
NASA's Artemis II crew passed the halfway mark to the Moon, releasing striking images of Earth from deep space. The mission marks the first crewed lunar voyage since Apollo 17 in 1972.

The images, described by the crew as "spectacular," have drawn wide attention as the mission builds toward a lunar flyby before returning to Earth.

sources.title: Artemis II crew now halfway to Moon as they take 'spectacular' image of Earth
NASA unveils first images of Earth from Artemis II

ai.headline: Anthropic Expands Aggressively While Claude Security Troubles Mount
ai.body:
Anthropic is moving fast on multiple fronts. The company acquired biotech startup Coefficient Bio for $400 million, launched a political action committee, and is riding high in private markets - though SpaceX's anticipated IPO may soon compete for the same investor dollars.

On the product side, Anthropic cut off OpenClaw's access to Claude Code subscriptions, forcing users to pay extra. The timing is awkward: OpenClaw is already under scrutiny after a security incident, with researchers advising users to assume their accounts are compromised. Separately, hackers are distributing a leaked Claude Code build bundled with malware.

Claude Code also made headlines for a genuine technical win - autonomously uncovering a Linux vulnerability that had gone undetected for 23 years.

sources.title: Hackers Are Posting the Claude Code Leak With Bonus Malware
Anthropic is having a moment in the private markets; SpaceX could spoil the party
Anthropic essentially bans OpenClaw from Claude by making subscribers pay extra
Claude Code Found a Linux Vulnerability Hidden for 23 Years
Tell HN: Anthropic no longer allowing Claude Code subscriptions to use OpenClaw
OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
Anthropic buys biotech startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: Reports
Anthropic ramps up its political activities with a new PAC

ai.headline: OpenAI Reshuffles Leadership as Key Executives Step Back
ai.body:
OpenAI is reorganizing at the top. COO Brad Lightcap moves into a new "special projects" role, while Fidji Simo, who joined less than a year ago to run the applications business, is taking medical leave. The head of OpenAI's AGI division is also stepping away.

The simultaneous departures raise questions about internal stability at a company already navigating a fraught transition from nonprofit to for-profit structure. OpenAI has not clarified who absorbs the responsibilities of the departing executives.

sources.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
OpenAI’s AGI boss is taking a leave of absence
OpenAI’s Fidji Simo Is Taking Medical Leave Amid an Executive Shake-Up

ai.headline: AI Is Eroding Users' Ability to Think for Themselves
ai.body:
New research finds that heavy AI users are increasingly willing to outsource their reasoning entirely to language models - a pattern researchers call "cognitive surrender." Participants shown AI-generated answers tended to accept them without scrutiny, even when the answers were wrong.

The finding lands as a separate cultural backlash takes shape. Creators and brands are now advertising work as "human-made" and "AI-free" as a selling point, suggesting a growing market for authenticity. The Indie Internet Index, a new directory of independent websites, reflects the same impulse - a desire to find corners of the web that feel genuinely human.

sources.title: The Indie Internet Index – submit your favorite sites
Really, you made this without AI? Prove it
"Cognitive surrender" leads AI users to abandon logical thinking, research finds

ai.headline: Musk Ties SpaceX IPO Banks to Grok Subscriptions
ai.body:
Elon Musk is reportedly requiring investment banks competing for SpaceX's IPO mandate to purchase Grok subscriptions, effectively using one of the most coveted deals in private markets as leverage to drive revenue to his AI venture xAI. The move blurs the line between business competition and coercion.

Meanwhile, Trump's broader AI infrastructure push is stalling. His own tariffs are raising the cost of hardware needed to build data centers, undermining the policy goals his administration has publicly championed. AI companies are filling some of the gap by constructing dedicated natural gas plants - a workaround that brings its own environmental and regulatory risks.

sources.title: Elon Musk insists banks working on SpaceX IPO must buy Grok subscriptions
Trump ignores biggest reasons his AI data center buildout is failing
AI companies are building huge natural gas plants to power data centers. What could go wrong?

btc.headline: Bitcoin Bears Face $2.5B Wipeout as Market Thins
btc.body:
Bitcoin short sellers are sitting on $2.5 billion in positions that would be liquidated if price climbs to $72,000 - a level analysts say is increasingly plausible. The setup is precarious for bears, especially as consolidation patterns historically precede sharp directional moves.

Underneath the surface, five separate data sources point to the same problem: Bitcoin's market liquidity is thinning. Fewer large orders are sitting in the book, meaning any significant buying pressure could trigger outsized price swings. That dynamic cuts both ways, but with short exposure this concentrated, a squeeze would hurt bears hardest.

Large traders are already bleeding. Wealthy Bitcoin holders lost an average of $337 million per day in Q1 2026, suggesting the current range has been grinding down even the most capitalized players.

sources.title: Bitcoin shorts risk $2.5 billion liquidation at $72K: Are bears in danger?
Five data sources say the same thing about bitcoin market. It's thinning from the inside
Rich Bitcoin traders lost $337M daily in first quarter of 2026
Bitcoin’s ‘no direction’ action may lead to heavier breakout: Analyst

btc.headline: Kalshi Blocked in Nevada as Prediction Markets Face Global Pushback
btc.body:
A Nevada judge extended the ban on Kalshi's sports event contracts, rejecting the platform's argument that its products are federally regulated derivatives rather than unlicensed gambling. The ruling keeps Kalshi out of Nevada and signals that state gambling authorities are not ready to cede ground to CFTC-regulated prediction markets.

The legal friction is not limited to the US. Prediction market platforms are running into similar resistance across strict Asian jurisdictions, where regulators are drawing hard lines between financial instruments and gambling products. The core legal question - whether event contracts are commodities or bets - remains unresolved in most markets.

Polymarket added to the industry's reputational pressure this week by pulling a market tied to a missing US pilot following public backlash, highlighting the ethical boundaries these platforms are still working out.

sources.title: Nevada judge extends ban on Kalshi, rejects event contract defense
Prediction markets are testing legal limits in strict Asian markets
Polymarket takes down market on missing US pilot after backlash
Judge continues Nevada ban on Kalshi sports markets

btc.headline: 24/7 Stock Trading Arrives, Borrowing a Page from Crypto
btc.body:
Traditional equity markets are moving toward round-the-clock trading, a shift that traders argue will eliminate the after-hours price gaps that have long allowed manipulation of opening prices. The change mirrors how crypto markets have operated since inception.

For crypto advocates, the move validates a core argument: that continuous markets are fairer and more efficient. The practical winner is the active trader who can now react to news in real time rather than waiting for a bell.

sources.title: Traders are the big winners as 24/7 stocks will finally end the after-hours price 'manipulation'

btc.headline: Solana Faces Speed Penalty to Harden Against Quantum Attacks
btc.body:
Solana's engineers are confronting an uncomfortable tradeoff as they assess quantum-resistant cryptography: the post-quantum algorithms that would protect the network are significantly slower than current ones, threatening the high throughput that defines Solana's competitive edge.

The dilemma is not unique to Solana, but it hits harder here because speed is the product. Adopting stronger cryptographic standards could degrade performance enough to undermine the network's core value proposition. No timeline for a fix has been announced, and the tension between security and speed has no clean resolution yet.

Separately, Tether is weighing whether to delay a fundraising round if investor demand falls short of supporting a $500 billion valuation, a sign that even the stablecoin giant is navigating a more cautious capital environment.

sources.title: Solana's quantum-threat readiness reveals harsh tradeoff: security vs speed
Tether may delay fundraising if demand falls short at $500B valuation: Report

food.name: Miso-Glazed Leek and Mushroom Galette
food.description: A free-form savory tart with caramelized leeks and shiitake mushrooms lacquered in white miso butter on a flaky rough-puff pastry base.
food.tip: Chill your butter cubes in the freezer for 15 minutes before making the pastry — visible butter flakes in the dough are a feature, not a flaw.

exercise.name: The Saturday Deck of Cards
exercise.equipment: A standard deck of playing cards, a mat
exercise.description: Assign a bodyweight movement to each suit — push-ups, squats, lunges, hollow-body holds — then flip cards one at a time and perform the number of reps shown, turning a random deck into an unpredictable full-body circuit.

learn.description: Stochastic resonance is the counterintuitive phenomenon where adding a precise amount of random noise to a weak signal actually makes that signal easier to detect — used in neuroscience, physics, and even hearing-aid design.
learn.why: It upends the assumption that noise is always the enemy of clarity, and once you understand it you start seeing its fingerprints everywhere from how neurons fire to why slightly wobbly balance boards improve proprioception in athletes.
